title: Tongoloa loloensis
taxonomy: Tongoloa loloensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Tongoloa, which is a group of plants within the parsley family. Tongoloa loloensis is further classified into the tribe Oenantheae and the subfamily Apioideae. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics. Further research is needed to confirm the taxonomic status of Tongoloa loloensis.

intro: Tongoloa loloensis, also known as Tongoloa, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Tongoloa loloensis include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Tongoloa is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to relieve symptoms of colds, coughs, and flu.
